libraryPreparationPreflight.agent_guidance: Use the 2.x API: RenderingContext was replaced by DocumentRenderingContext, SkinResourceLoader and the old logging/default-template setters are gone. Prefer looking up/injecting SiteRenderer through a Plexus/Sisu test container rather than calling new DefaultSiteRenderer() for render/locateDocumentFiles paths. Build small temporary site content and a SiteRenderingContext/SiteModel in test code; no Docker image, environment variable, or system property is indicated.

deterministic_setup.reason: doxia-site-renderer is a Plexus/Sisu component with injected collaborators; upstream 2.0.0 tests use Plexus testing support to obtain an injected SiteRenderer instead of constructing DefaultSiteRenderer directly.

The renderer needs a Doxia parser module on the test classpath to render common APT site documents; the 2.0.0 POM notes parser modules are no longer in doxia-core and must be declared so their component descriptors are picked up.

input_evidence.name: issue
input_evidence.summary: #8134 [Automation] javac compile fails for org.apache.maven.doxia:doxia-site-renderer:2.0.0; label=fails-javac-compile; body_chars=8015

risks: The existing 1.9.1 tests are API-stale and will not compile against 2.0.0 without being rewritten.
Tests that only call copyResources or simple constructors may compile without extra dependencies but are likely shallow compared with actual site rendering coverage.
If tests choose xdoc or fml inputs instead of APT, matching doxia-module-xdoc or doxia-module-fml dependencies would be needed instead.
libraryPreparationPreflight.summary: Meaningful doxia-site-renderer coverage normally exercises rendering through Plexus/Sisu injection and a Doxia input parser; the resolved 2.0.0 artifact only brings the XHTML5 output module transitively, while upstream declares APT parser support only as a test dependency.
